phase_1_rationale: Candidate B cleanly isolates the GraphQL query changes to the items queries by introducing a new DTO, leaving the base `Project` struct and unrelated queries untouched. Candidate A unnecessarily modifies the shared `Project` struct, forcing invasive changes and O(N) conversions in unrelated project list functions.

phase_1_semantic_concerns.A: Modifies the base `Project` struct to include a query parameter, requiring invasive changes to unrelated functions (`UserProjects`, `OrgProjects`, etc.) and introducing an O(N) allocation loop to map DTOs back to `Project`s. Reliance on embedded structs (`projectDTOBase`) could also be brittle with GraphQL reflection.
phase_1_semantic_concerns.B: Fails to wrap the HTTP client with `api.NewCachedHTTPClient` for feature detection, which will cause an un-cached GraphQL introspection request on every command invocation using `--query`.
